prompt: Construct a HarborOps Offline Logistics & Care Transit Suite supporting regional senior-care operators that manage both critical asset storage and scheduled group transportation without relying on the internet. The React web interface provides role-based entry points for Seniors, Family Members, Caregivers, Organization Admins, and Platform Admins, with screen-level authorization so a Family Member can book and track trips but cannot view warehouse counts, while an Org Admin can manage both. Users register with a username and password (minimum 12 characters, at least one letter and one number) and complete real-name and credential verification through an offline review flow where approved staff validate uploaded ID/credential images and attestations; sensitive identifiers are displayed masked by default. Caregivers and admins can publish, edit, and unpublish group trips as drafts or live versions, configuring an origin, optional waypoints, service date and pickup time windows (for example, 03/25/2026 between 8: 00 AM–9: 00 AM), a headcount limit, pricing rules in USD (flat fare or per-seat), and a signup deadline that must be at least 2 hours before departure; when a trip is updated after signups, the UI clearly labels it as a new version and requires re-acknowledgment from affected riders. The same interface supports warehouse master data setup across multiple warehouses, zones, and locations, including capacity limits and attributes such as temperature zone and hazmat class, plus owner/supplier/carrier records with effective-dated versions. For inventory operations, the UI lets admins create inventory plans by organization, region, and asset type for spot counts or full counts, distribute tasks to staff, and monitor progress dashboards that highlight variances and require a structured review and closure confirmation. Users also manage profiles and preferences, including frequent traveler profiles with encrypted/masked IDs, favorites and comparisons, local subscription alerts shown as in-app reminders, self-service data export, and account deletion with an explicit retention notice. The backend uses Django to implement a REST-style API layer that the React client calls over HTTPS on the local network, with MySQL as the system of record for accounts, roles, trips, warehouses/locations, inventory plans, and audit trails. Crawl orchestration is implemented as an offline job framework for ingesting local manifests and attachments (for example, scanning designated on-prem folders for CSV updates and images), supporting manual and scheduled triggers, job priorities and dependencies, per-organization concurrency limits (default 3 concurrent jobs), exponential retry backoff (1 min, 5 min, 15 min, then fail), failure isolation per job, resumable progress checkpoints, and horizontal scaling by running additional worker processes on other on-prem machines that share the same MySQL queue tables. Inventory variance closure is enforced server-side by comparing book quantities to physical counts and categorizing issues as missing, extra, or data mismatch; any variance above 2 units or 1% (whichever is higher) is automatically routed to a reviewer, requires an accountability acknowledgment, and cannot be closed until a corrective action is recorded and approved. Security protections include request signing with timestamps and nonces to prevent replay (5-minute validity window), CSRF protection for browser sessions, input validation and parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection, output encoding to reduce XSS risk, and rate limiting with an offline CAPTCHA challenge after 5 failed login attempts; accounts lock for 15 minutes after 10 consecutive failures. Sensitive fields (government ID, credential numbers, and traveler identifiers) are encrypted at rest using AES-256 with a locally stored key, and all displays use least-privilege masking unless a privileged role explicitly justifies access. The platform supports local backup and recovery with nightly encrypted database dumps retained for 30 days, plus anomaly alerts surfaced inside the admin console (for example, repeated failed logins, unusual job failure spikes, or bulk exports) without any external notification dependencies.
